title: How French courts became Europe's toughest on women returning from IS
subhed: Of the 23 terrorism trials scheduled in French courts in 2026, 18 involve women who joined the Islamic State organization in Syria. While their role was once overlooked, they are now being judged on equal footing with men.

Ever-extreme Eric Zemmour prepares second bid for French president
subhed: For a time, Zemmour's partner, Sarah Knafo, had positioned herself as a potential candidate for the couple's party, Reconquête. But the far-right polemicist is claiming the limelight again.

title: Bold new proposal calls for merging Paris with suburbs
subhed: Clément Beaune, the French high commissioner for strategy and planning, proposed a sweeping overhaul of the Greater Paris area and its local governments.

title: Artist Louise Vo Tan brings the Louvre's stolen crown jewels back to life
subhed: 'They were dazzling, they looked like a meringue,' said Vo Tan, who filmed the museum's treasures before they were stolen and is now displaying them in hologram form in the building once used for the Port-Coton foghorn.

title: Marjane Satrapi, powerful and outspoken artist who swore to 'stay true to herself'
subhed: Famous for her four-part graphic novel 'Persepolis' recounting her childhood in Iran during the Islamic Revolution, which she also adapted for the screen, Satrapi was also later involved with the Woman, Life, Freedom movement.

HSBC charged for helping former Lebanese central bank head embezzle hundreds of millions
subhed: The Swiss subsidiary of HSBC is suspected of having helped former Lebanese central bank chief Riad Salameh embezzle more than $300 million from the country's central bank. The charges mark a significant development in the French judicial investigation.

title: Marjane Satrapi: 'At age 10, I was training myself to become a political prisoner'
subhed: The Franco-Iranian artist, who gained worldwide recognition with the graphic novel 'Persepolis,' died at 56 years old, her family announced Thursday. Le Monde interviewed her in October 2020. We are republishing that interview today.

title: Bayeux Tapestry's transport to British Museum will be safe, detailed study determines
subhed: Before the fragile 70-meter-long embroidery is transported to the United Kingdom in July, a new risk assessment study released on Wednesday has given the move the green light.
